title: Generic exceptions should never be thrown
description: This rule raises an issue when a generic exception (such as std::exception, std::logic_error or std::runtime_error) is thrown. Throwing generic exceptions such as std::exception, std::logic_error and std::runtime_error will have a negative impact on any code trying to catch these exceptions. From a consumer perspective, it is generally a best practice to only catch exceptions you intend to handle.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
